issues.message: runCli is imported from dist/cli.js but is never exported from src/cli.ts — this will throw a runtime error whenever the CLI is invoked with arguments
issues.suggestion: Export a runCli function from src/cli.ts that calls parseArgs and dispatches to the appropriate command handler, or update the import to use the exported parseArgs function directly

issues.message: Multiple type assertions (as Record<string, unknown>) are used to access unknown API response data instead of leveraging the Zod dependency already present in the project
issues.suggestion: Define Zod schemas for API response shapes (PushResult, TeamResponse, TaskUpdateResult, ErrorResponse) and use safeParse for runtime validation, eliminating all four as-cast parse functions

issues.message: statusRaw and phaseRaw are validated manually then cast with as TaskStatus and as TaskPhase rather than using the existing VALID_STATUSES and VALID_PHASES const arrays as a type-safe guard
issues.suggestion: Use a type predicate function (e.g. isTaskStatus(v: string): v is TaskStatus) backed by the const tuple to avoid the type assertion after the includes check, consistent with the pattern already used in src/core/parser.ts

issues.message: orphanFeatures is computed via a filter but immediately discarded — the comment acknowledges this is intentional but the allocation is wasted and confusing
issues.suggestion: Remove the orphanFeatures variable and its filter call entirely, or replace with a debug-mode logging path. The epicIds Set is only needed to build this unused variable, so both can be removed if silent dropping is the intended contract

issues.message: Deep equality of the meta field is determined by JSON.stringify comparison, which is sensitive to property insertion order and will produce false positives for semantically identical objects with different key orderings
issues.suggestion: Use a structural deep-equality helper (e.g. a recursive object comparison or a lightweight library like fast-deep-equal) to compare meta objects, or normalise the key order before serialisation

issues.message: The MCP server module is an empty stub that exits with code 1 — there is no documentation indicating when this feature will be delivered or what the interface contract is
issues.suggestion: Add a JSDoc comment to startMcpServer documenting the expected MCP protocol, the list of tools to be implemented, and a reference to the tracking issue or PRD feature ID so consumers understand the intended contract

issues.message: The isKnownCommand type predicate uses an as Command cast inside the Set.has call to satisfy TypeScript — the same pattern is used again on line 36 when assigning command, resulting in two assertions for one value
issues.suggestion: Change KNOWN_COMMANDS to Set<string> (since Command is a union of string literals) and use a single type predicate that avoids the internal cast, consistent with the isTaskStatus pattern in parser.ts
